content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS with length NNN. You should find two non-intersecting substrings of SSS such that the second one is a substring of the first one and the product of these substrings' lengths is the maximum possible. More formally, let's denote a contiguous substring Sl,Sl+1,‚Ä¶,SrS_l, S_{l+1}, \ldots, S_rSl‚Äã,Sl+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Sr‚Äã by S[l,r]S[l, r]S[l,r]; you should find integers l1l_1l1‚Äã, r1r_1r1‚Äã, l2l_2l2‚Äã and r2r_2r2‚Äã which satisfy these four criteria:


1‚â§l1‚â§r1‚â§N1 \le l_1 \le r_1 \le N1‚â§l1‚Äã‚â§r1‚Äã‚â§N and 1‚â§l2‚â§r2‚â§N1 \le l_2 \le r_2 \le N1‚â§l2‚Äã‚â§r2‚Äã‚â§N
r2<l1r_2 \lt l_1r2‚Äã<l1‚Äã or r1<l2r_1 \lt l_2r1‚Äã<l2‚Äã
S[l2,r2]S[l_2, r_2]S[l2‚Äã,r2‚Äã] is a contiguous substring of S[l1,r1]S[l_1, r_1]S[l1‚Äã,r1‚Äã]
the product P=(r2‚àíl2+1)‚ãÖ(r1‚àíl1+1)P = (r_2-l_2+1) \cdot (r_1-l_1+1)P=(r2‚Äã‚àíl2‚Äã+1)‚ãÖ(r1‚Äã‚àíl1‚Äã+1) is maximum possible
Find the maximum value of PPP. It is guaranteed that for the given test data, there is at least one way to choose l1,r1,l2,r2l_1, r_1, l_2, r_2l1‚Äã,r1‚Äã,l2‚Äã,r2‚Äã such that the first three criteria are satisfied, i.e. the answer is well-defined.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
`ExplanationWe can choose substrings S[1,2]S[1, 2]S[1,2] and S[3,5]S[3, 5]S[3,5].
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a single string SSS with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the maximum product PPP.
